body:
Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base voice cloning (the default in-context-learning path, `task_type=Base` with `ref_audio` and no extra flags) intermittently generates no EOS on current `main` (post the vLLM 0.24 rebase in #4709) and runs to `max_tokens` (4096 at 12Hz, ~318.8s of garbled, looping audio) instead of stopping normally. A single isolated request with no concurrency reproduces it, so this is not a load or preemption issue.

Found while re-verifying [#4559](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/pull/4559) on 0.24. Ruled out along the way, with direct instrumentation rather than guesswork:

- Not #4559 itself: a single request never triggers preemption, so its resume-replay code path is never exercised.
- Not a config/mask problem: the talker's `codec_eos_token_id` loads correctly (2150, in-range), and the logit mask allows it to be sampled.
- Not a transformers package regression: pinning the venv down to `transformers==5.5.3` (the floor vLLM 0.24 allows) reproduces the identical runaway.
- Not the known transformers 5.9 `create_causal_mask`/`cache_position` shim in the codec tokenizer: the reference-audio encoder that shim guards is exercised here, but its output codes are healthy (full codebook range, no degeneracy) when instrumented directly.

What actually isolates it: the same request with `x_vector_only_mode: true` (skips in-context conditioning, uses the explicit per-step `trailing_text`/`talker_text_offset` streaming signal instead) completes correctly in a few seconds. So the bug is specific to the ICL pattern, where the whole reference + text is baked into the prefill and the model is expected to recognize completion purely through attention over that prefill, with no explicit decode-time signal.

Further isolating within that: setting `enforce_eager: true` on stage-0 makes the same ICL request complete correctly too. The prefill here is well under `max_num_batched_tokens`, so chunked prefill was never actually the variable, and disabling it alone did nothing; `enforce_eager` alone reproduces the fix. That points at vLLM 0.24's CUDA-graph/torch.compile-compiled forward path for this stage, not at vllm-omni's own prompt-construction code in `qwen3_tts_talker.py` / `prompt_embeds_builder.py`.

I have not bisected the exact vLLM-internals mechanism (would need to diff 0.23 vs 0.24 compilation/cudagraph handling for this backbone), and I'd rather not ship `enforce_eager` broadly as the fix since it costs the graph/compile speedup for every voice-clone request, not just the affected case. Flagging this now given @Sy0307's recent work on the qwen3_tts_talker resume path, in case this overlaps with something already in progress or narrower to fix from that side. Happy to share the exact repro script and instrumentation if useful.

comments.author: linyueqian
comments.body:
Steps to reproduce, using the repo's own reference fixture:

1. Serve on current `main` (vLLM 0.24, default deploy config, single GPU):

```bash
vllm-omni serve Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base \
  --deploy-config vllm_omni/deploy/qwen3_tts.yaml \
  --trust-remote-code --omni \
  --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8902
```

2. Send one isolated voice-clone request (no concurrency), using `tests/assets/qwen3_tts/clone_2.wav` from this repo as the reference audio:

```python
import base64, requests

with open("tests/assets/qwen3_tts/clone_2.wav", "rb") as f:
    ref_audio = "data:audio/wav;base64," + base64.b64encode(f.read()).decode()

payload = {
    "model": "Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base",
    "input": "Hello, my name is Sarah and I work at the public library downtown.",
    "response_format": "wav",
    "task_type": "Base",
    "voice": "clone",
    "ref_audio": ref_audio,
    "ref_text": "Okay. Yeah. I resent you. I love you. I respect you. But you know what? You blew it! And thanks to you.",
}
r = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:8902/v1/audio/speech", json=payload, timeout=400)
print(r.status_code, len(r.content), "bytes ->", len(r.content) / 2 / 24000, "s")
```

Expected: a few seconds of audio speaking the input text.
Actual (intermittent, but reliably reproducible over a handful of tries): 15302444 bytes = 318.8s, i.e. `max_tokens` (4096 at 12Hz) with no EOS. Transcribing the output shows degenerate stuttering ("Hello. Hello. My name. My.") instead of the input sentence.

To confirm it's specific to the ICL/reference-audio-conditioning path, add `"x_vector_only_mode": true` to the payload: the same request then completes correctly in a few seconds.

To confirm it's the compiled/CUDA-graph forward and not chunked prefill, pass `--stage-overrides '{"0": {"enforce_eager": true}}'` on the serve command (default chunked-prefill setting unchanged): the original (no `x_vector_only_mode`) request then also completes correctly.

comments.author: linyueqian
comments.body:
Closing this. Re-verified from scratch on a clean environment: fresh `git fetch` of current `main` (`e4a2d367`), fresh editable install, same server config and same request as the repro above, 35 back-to-back trials, all completed correctly (3.4-4.9s each), zero runaways. Also tested against [#4559](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/pull/4559)'s current head (`289107b1`) with the same rigor: 20/20 clean. 55/55 total across both branches.

@Sy0307 was right that this doesn't reproduce. I don't have a confirmed explanation for what I originally saw; the most likely one is that my test session had been running for hours with repeated code patches, dependency version changes, and at one point shared GPU usage with another process, and something in that state (not a clean 0.24/main regression) produced the runaway. I can't rule out it was a one-off fluke either.

Environment for reference, in case anyone hits something similar: Ubuntu 22.04.5, Python 3.12.13, torch 2.11.0+cu129, vLLM 0.24.0, transformers 5.12.1 (also reproduced-then-not on 5.5.3), GPU reports as `NVIDIA L20X` via `nvidia-smi` but the CUDA runtime reports compute capability 9.0 / 132 SMs / ~150GB, consistent with H200-class Hopper silicon rather than a real L20.

Apologies for the noise. Closing as not reproducible; happy to reopen if it recurs with a clean repro.

title: [Bug]: Qwen3-TTS Base voice-clone (ICL) generation runs to max_tokens without EOS on vLLM 0.24
